heading: Family leave policies for the Coast Guard
label: 2512 Family leave policies for the Coast Guard
notes: Amendments2025—, substituted “Family leave” for “Leave” in section catchline.Pub. L. 119–60, § 7225(a)(1)Subsec. L. 115–282, title III, § 315(b)132 Stat. 4250Dec. 4, 2018“Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating shall ensure that a flexible work schedule program under chapter 61 of title 5, United States Code, is in place for officers and enlisted members of the Coast Guard.”Dec. 4, 2018

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), not later than 1 year after the date on which the Secretary of the Navy promulgates a new rule, policy, or memorandum pursuant to or, with respect to the reserve component of the Coast Guard, the Secretary of Defense promulgates a new regulation for members of the reserve component of the Coast Guard pursuant to , with respect to leave associated with the birth or placement of a minor child with the member for adoption or long term foster care, the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating shall promulgate a similar rule, policy, or memorandum that provides leave to officers, enlisted members, and members of the reserve component of the Coast Guard that is equal in duration and compensation to that provided by the Secretary of the Navy or, with respect to members of the reserve component of the Coast Guard, the Secretary of Defense.section 704 of title 10section 711 of title 10

children.heading: .—Leave Associated With Birth or Placement of Minor Child With Member for Adoption or Long Term Foster Care
children.label: b .—Leave Associated With Birth or Placement of Minor Child With Member for Adoption or Long Term Foster Care

children.text: to take such leave in increments; and
to use flexible work schedules (pursuant to a program established by the Secretary in accordance with chapter 61 of title 5).

children.label: C
children.text: other circumstances that the Secretary determines reasonable and appropriate.

children.heading: .—Extended deadline
children.label: 2 .—Extended deadline
children.text: The regulation, rule, policy, or memorandum prescribed under paragraph (a) shall require that any leave authorized to be taken after the one-year period described in subparagraph (c)(1)(A) shall be taken within a reasonable period of time, as determined by the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating, after cessation of the circumstances warranting the extended deadline.12

children.heading: .—Member of the Reserve Component of the Coast Guard Defined
children.label: d .—Member of the Reserve Component of the Coast Guard Defined

children.text: the selected reserve who is entitled to compensation under ; orsection 206 of title 37

the individual ready reserve who is entitled to compensation under when attending or participating in a sufficient number of periods of inactive-duty training during a year to count the year as a qualifying year of creditable service toward eligibility for retired pay.section 206 of title 37
